LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT HEBREW HOME FOR THE AGED DISABLED'S DEMURRER TO PORTIONS OF PLAINTIFFS' COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. A COMPLAINT MUST ALLEGE THE FACTS GIVING RISE TO AN ELDER ABUSE CAUSE OF ACTION WITH PARTICULARITY. (CARTER V. HEALTHCARE PARADISE VALLEY LLC (2011) 198 CAL.APP.4TH 396, 407, "THE FACTS CONSTITUTING THE NEGLECT AND ESTABLISHING THE CAUSAL LINK BETWEEN THE NEGLECT AND THE INJURY MUST BE PLEADED WITH PARTICULARITY, IN ACCORDANCE WITH THE PLEADING RULES GOVERNING STATUTORY CLAIMS."). ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK FELICIA GREEN; COURT REPORTER MELANIE GHENO, CSR NO. 7489; DEPT. 302. (302/EHG)

ORDER REGARDING DEFENDANT HEBREW HOME FOR THE AGED DISBALED'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF PLAINTIFFS' SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT HEBREW HOME FOR THE AGED DISABLED'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT, DEFENDANT HEBREW HOME'S DEMURRER TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR VIOLATION OF THE ELDER ABUSE AND DEPENDENT ADULT CIVIL PROTECTION ACT IN THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH TWENTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. WHILE PLAINTIFFS HAVE SUFFICIENTLY PLED THAT DEFENDANT'S EMPLOYEES RECKLESSLY COMMITTED ELDER ABUSE, PLAINTIFFS HAVE NOT ADEQUATELY ALLEGED RATIFICATION OF THAT ABUSE BY AN OFFICER, DIRECTOR OR MANAGING AGENT OF DEFENDANT. BECAUSE IT IS POSSIBLE THAT PLAINTIFFS MAY BE ABLE TO SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGE RATIFICATION, PLAINTIFF IS GIVEN LEAVE TO AMEND HER RATIFICATION ALLEGATIONS TO CONFORM TO THE REQUIREMENTS OF CIVIL CODE 3294(B) IF SHE CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Sep-13-2016 Off Calendar

Petition To Approve Compromise Of Pending Action
Off Calendar Aug-23-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for a Hearing on a Minor's Compromise.
Department 514, Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: A. Mok, CSR: Mitch Lyon, CSR#6840.

Carey Wood, Esq., specially appearing for Petitioner: Vicki Michelle Brown, mother and guardian ad litem for Minor: Laila Charisse Colleen Foster present in court.
Petitioner sworn and examined.

After testimony and consideration of the evidence presented, the Petition is granted and compromise is approved as follows:
Total Settlement: $28,124.98
Attorney's Fees: $8,519.55 payable to Law Firm of Carpenter, Zuckerman & Rowley, LLP
Costs: $1,319.76
A net balance of $18,645.67 to be deposited into a blocked account at:
Wells Fargo, 340 E. Round Grove Road, Lewisville, Texas 75067 until Minor reaches the age of 18 years.

Court signed Order Granting approval of Minor and Order to Deposit Money into blocked Account.
Status Hearing Scheduled on November 29, 2016 at 9:00 AM, in Department 514 for filing of receipt for the deposit of money into blocked account.
No appearance is required if the receipt is filed and courtesy copy lodged with Dept. 514 prior to Hearing.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-04-2016 Continued To May-25-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-03-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. KYLE P. SCHNEBERG, ESQ. of Carpenter, Zuckerman & Rowley, LLP, Tel No. 310-273-1230 appearing telephonically for the Plaintiff;
Atty. JENNIFER E. ELGIE, ESQ. of Lewis Brisbois Bisgaard & Smith LLP, Tel No. 415-438-6610 appearing for Defendant Hebrew Home for the Aged Disabled.

Matter was not argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. As to DEFENDANT HEBREW HOME FOR THE AGED DISABLED'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT, Defendant Hebrew Home's demurrer to the first cause of action for violation of the Elder Abuse and Dependent Adult Civil Protection Act in the second amended complaint is sustained with twenty days leave to amend. While plaintiffs have sufficiently pled that defendant's employees recklessly committed elder abuse, plaintiffs have not adequately alleged ratification of that abuse by an officer, director or managing agent of defendant. Because it is possible that plaintiffs may be able to sufficiently allege ratification, plaintiff is given leave to amend her ratification allegations to conform to the requirements of Civil Code 3294(b) if she can do so in good faith. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

Defendant Hebrew Home For The Aged Disableds Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs Second Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Mar-03-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. KYLE P. SCHNEBERG, ESQ. of Carpenter, Zuckerman & Rowley, LLP, Tel No. 310-273-1230 appearing telephonically for the Plaintiff;
Atty. JENNIFER E. ELGIE, ESQ. of Lewis Brisbois Bisgaard & Smith LLP, Tel No. 415-438-6610 appearing for Defendant Hebrew Home for the Aged Disabled.

Matter was not argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Defendant Hebrew Home For The Aged Disableds' Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs Second Amended Complaint is off calendar in light of the ruling on defendant's demurrer. For future guidance of the parties, the court prefers that parties not file two separate motions raising the same issues. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-04-2016 Continued To Mar-23-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-01-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JENNIFER E. ELGIE OF THE LEWIS BRISBOIS LLP, TEL. NO. 1-415-438-6610 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT HEBREW HOME FOR THE AGED DISABLED'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is denied as moot, in light of the ruling on demurrer. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/ehg

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-01-2015 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JENNIFER E. ELGIE OF THE LEWIS BRISBOIS LLP, TEL. NO. 1-415-438-6610 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT HEBREW HOME FOR THE AGED DISABLED'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS sustained, with twenty days leave to amend. Plaintiffs have pled facts that sufficiently allege a cause of action for elder abuse. Nevertheless, Defendant's demurrer to the first amended complaint is sustained, with twenty days leave to amend, to provide Plaintiffs with an opportunity to plead facts that a specific officer, director, or managing agent of Hebrew Home for the Aged and Disabled authorized, ratified, or engaged in any of the alleged abuse or hired the employee responsible for the abuse with advance knowledge of the person's unfitness and with a conscious disregard of the rights and safety of others. (Welfare and Institutions Code section 15657(c) and Civil Code section 3294.). Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/ehg

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-21-2015 Continued To Oct-28-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Defendant Hebrew Home For The Aged Disabled Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stirke Portions Of Plaintiffs Complaint
Off Calendar Jul-20-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Jennifer E. Elgie, of Lewis Brisbois Bisgaard & Smith LLP, Tel. 415.438.6610, for defendants;
No other appearances.

Ruling: The matter was not argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant Hebrew Home For The Aged Disabled's Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs' Complaint is denied as moot, in light of the ruling on demurrer. Order signed in open court. Judge Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk Felicia Green; Court Reporter Melanie Gheno, CSR No. 7489; Dept. 302. (302/EHG)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-20-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Jennifer E. Elgie, of Lewis Brisbois Bisgaard & Smith LLP, Tel. 415.438.6610, for defendants;
No other appearances.

Ruling: The matter was not argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows:
DEFENDANT HEBREW HOME FOR THE AGED DISABLED'S DEMURRER TO PORTIONS OF PLAINTIFFS' COMPLAINT is sustained with 10 days leave to amend. A complaint must allege the facts giving rise to an Elder Abuse cause of action with particularity. (Carter v. Healthcare Paradise Valley LLC (2011) 198 Cal.App.4th 396, 407, "the facts constituting the neglect and establishing the causal link between the neglect and the injury must be pleaded with particularity, in accordance with the pleading rules governing statutory claims."). Order signed in open court. Judge Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk Felicia Green; Court Reporter Melanie Gheno, CSR No. 7489; Dept. 302. (302/EHG)
